_comment: Win-rate acceptance band for scripts/selfplay.mjs --check. Content changes must keep the simulated win rate inside [min,max] and strictly beatable (>0) and survivable (<1). Regenerate the observed numbers with: node scripts/selfplay.mjs --runs 6000 --check. The exhaustive per-content history lives in scripts/content-backlog.json (one detailed entry per shipped module); this file keeps only the current snapshot plus the balance method.

observed.note: MECHANIC-TELEGRAPHS (2026-07-17, LADDER 2 #17, [UI]): BALANCE UNCHANGED — and proven so rather than assumed. This module is pure presentation: it surfaces the five apex mechanics (enrage/ward/thorns/sunder/execute) as one-line battle-log callouts (span.telegraph, fired from js/hero.js interact() on the same 400ms cadence as the damage numbers, deduped to ONCE PER FIGHT by a `told` map) plus a realm-arrival biome banner (span.banner) in scene.levelUp via lj.realm.getBiomeName(). The ONLY touch to combat.js is stamping the previously-missing `.sundered` flag on the boss's log entry (`if (defender !== heroStats)`, the same identity idiom the existing `attacker !== enemy` enrage/execute flag uses) — no math, no RNG. PROOF: seeded lj.util.randomInterval with mulberry32 (it is the only RNG combat consumes) and diffed the full outcome+damage+type stream over 4,200 fights spanning every grade (a/b/c/d/B/T/W/S/M/H) x realms 1-9 against HEAD's combat.js — BIT-IDENTICAL. SELFPLAY-BLIND BY CONSTRUCTION: playthrough() re-implements the boss loop and never calls interact() or scene.levelUp(), so no harness mirror was needed and the win rate is structurally untouched. Shipped `node scripts/selfplay.mjs --runs 6000 --check` read 37.8% (2269/6000) IN-BAND [25,75], beatable+survivable (outcomes legendary-set 2269 / died-boss 2797 / died-room 934, avg realm 7.13, deepest 10), structural green (26,400 rooms, 2,000 boss arenas; biomes halls 400 / frost 1600 / blight 3600 / ember 20800) — statistically level with the prior 36.7% (boss-lair-arena) — a ~1.1pt gap is ~1.8 sigma at n=6000 and selfplay is UNSEEDED, so it is run-to-run noise, which the bit-identical proof above independently guarantees. JS-all 31.82KB brotli (<=55KB), CSS 1.36KB (<=5KB) after two new colour classes. Browser-gated by a NEW spec (tests/playtest/telegraphs.spec.ts, 3 tests — selfplay cannot see any of this): all five callouts render with correct text + class and an unknown flag stays silent; realms 2-5 announce the right biome through the real levelUp path; and a real end-to-end Molten Reaver fight telegraphs sunder EXACTLY ONCE (the Reaver fires on its first swing in 100% of fights, so the test is fast and non-flaky, and it covers the new flag + the dedupe). All three were MUTATION-TESTED to fail when their target was broken. Full committed suite 10/10 green. MEASUREMENT GOTCHA worth keeping: do NOT set lj.hero.stats.health = 1e9 to make a probe hero survive — execute compares the hero against its REAL max (100+hp*5), so an above-max health makes execute unfireable and reads as a false negative; use stats.heal(1e9) (caps at max). Likewise a gearless probe hero almost never triggers ENRAGE (0.8% at realm 5) because it cannot wound the boss past its threshold — a mechanic that "never fires" in a probe is usually the probe, not the code. The realm-scaling exponent in lj.enemy.base (js/enemy.js) remains the primary GLOBAL difficulty lever. Regenerate with: node scripts/selfplay.mjs --runs 6000 --check
